audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua Judah boundary, Caleb-Debir, Achsah springs, city-list, and Jebusite-remnant register, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Judah's south/east/north/west borders, Hebron/Anakim, Debir conquest prize, Achsah's land-and-water request, regional city lists and totals, Philistine coastal cities, and Jerusalem's continuing Jebusite population.

verses.restored: Lot for tribe of sons of Judah according to their clans was toward border of Edom, wilderness of Zin southward, at extreme south.
Their south border was from end of Salt Sea, from bay that faces southward.
It went out south of ascent of Akrabbim, passed to Zin, went up south of Kadesh-barnea, passed Hezron, went up to Addar, and turned toward Karka.
It passed toward Azmon, went out to wadi of Egypt, and border's end was sea. This shall be your south border.
East border was Salt Sea as far as mouth of Jordan. Border on north side began from bay of sea at mouth of Jordan.
Border went up to Beth-hoglah, passed north of Beth-arabah, and went up to Stone of Bohan son of Reuben.
Border went up to Debir from Valley of Achor and northward toward Gilgal, opposite ascent of Adummim south of wadi; border passed to waters of En-shemesh and its end was at En-rogel.
Border went up by Valley of son of Hinnom to southern shoulder of Jebusite, that is Jerusalem; border went up to top of mountain facing Valley of Hinnom westward, at north end of Valley of Rephaim.
Border was marked from top of mountain to spring of waters of Nephtoah, went out to cities of Mount Ephron, and was marked to Baalah, that is Kiriath-jearim.
Border turned from Baalah westward to Mount Seir, passed to northern shoulder of Mount Jearim, that is Chesalon, went down to Beth-shemesh, and passed Timnah.
Border went out to northern shoulder of Ekron, was marked toward Shikkeron, passed Mount Baalah, and went out to Jabneel; border's end was sea.
West border was Great Sea and its coast. This was border around sons of Judah according to their clans.
To Caleb son of Jephunneh Joshua gave a portion in midst of sons of Judah according to command of YHWH to Joshua: Kiriath-arba, father of Anak, that is Hebron.
Caleb dispossessed from there three sons of Anak: Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai, children of Anak.
He went up from there against inhabitants of Debir; name of Debir formerly was Kiriath-sepher.
Caleb said, Whoever strikes Kiriath-sepher and captures it, I will give him Achsah my daughter as wife.
Othniel son of Kenaz, brother of Caleb, captured it, and he gave him Achsah his daughter as wife.
It was, when she came, that she urged him to ask from her father a field; then she dismounted from donkey, and Caleb said to her, What is for you?
She said, Give me a blessing, for you have given me land of Negev; give me also springs of water. So he gave her upper springs and lower springs.
This is inheritance of tribe of sons of Judah according to their clans.
Cities at extreme end of tribe of sons of Judah toward border of Edom in south were Kabzeel, Eder, Jagur,
Kinah, Dimonah, Adadah,
Kedesh, Hazor, Ithnan,
Ziph, Telem, Bealoth,
Hazor-hadattah, Kerioth-hezron, that is Hazor,
Amam, Shema, Moladah,
Hazar-gaddah, Heshmon, Beth-pelet,
Hazar-shual, Beer-sheba, Biziothiah,
Baalah, Iim, Ezem,
Eltolad, Chesil, Hormah,
Ziklag, Madmannah, Sansannah,
Lebaoth, Shilhim, Ain, and Rimmon; all cities twenty-nine, with their villages.
In Shephelah: Eshtaol, Zorah, Ashnah,
Zanoah, En-gannim, Tappuah, Enam,
Jarmuth, Adullam, Socoh, Azekah,
Shaaraim, Adithaim, Gederah, and Gederothaim; fourteen cities with their villages.
Zenan, Hadashah, Migdal-gad,
Dilean, Mizpeh, Joktheel,
Lachish, Bozkath, Eglon,
Cabbon, Lahmam, Chitlish,
Gederoth, Beth-dagon, Naamah, and Makkedah; sixteen cities with their villages.
Libnah, Ether, Ashan,
Iphtah, Ashnah, Nezib,
Keilah, Achzib, and Mareshah; nine cities with their villages.
Ekron, with its towns and villages;
from Ekron toward sea, all beside Ashdod, with their villages;
Ashdod, its towns and villages; Gaza, its towns and villages, as far as wadi of Egypt and Great Sea and its coast.
In hill country: Shamir, Jattir, Socoh,
Dannah, Kiriath-sannah, that is Debir,
Anab, Eshtemoh, Anim,
Goshen, Holon, and Giloh; eleven cities with their villages.
Arab, Dumah, Eshan,
Janim, Beth-tappuah, Aphekah,
Humtah, Kiriath-arba, that is Hebron, and Zior; nine cities with their villages.
Maon, Carmel, Ziph, Juttah,
Jezreel, Jokdeam, Zanoah,
Kain, Gibeah, and Timnah; ten cities with their villages.
Halhul, Beth-zur, Gedor,
Maarath, Beth-anoth, and Eltekon; six cities with their villages.
Kiriath-baal, that is Kiriath-jearim, and Rabbah; two cities with their villages.
In wilderness: Beth-arabah, Middin, Secacah,
Nibshan, City of Salt, and En-gedi; six cities with their villages.
But Jebusites, inhabitants of Jerusalem, sons of Judah were not able to dispossess; so Jebusite dwells with sons of Judah in Jerusalem to this day.

translation_notes: Judah's boundary description uses ancient landmarks, wadis, springs, ridges, and renamed towns whose exact identifications are often uncertain.
The border register is historical geography, not a modern political border or title document.
Caleb's capture of Hebron and Othniel's capture of Debir remain conquest stories involving existing Anakim and city populations.

Achsah is transferred as a marriage reward within a patriarchal arrangement, yet she speaks directly, requests a blessing, and secures both upper and lower springs. Her agency should be honored without romanticizing the system that treats her marriage as a prize.
Water transforms dry land into viable inheritance; Achsah's request reveals that land without resources is not a meaningful gift.
The city totals do not always align neatly with the names preserved in the Masoretic text, and Septuagintal witnesses include notable variants and additions, especially around verse 59.
Ekron, Ashdod, and Gaza appear in Judah's allotment despite continuing Philistine presence, showing the difference between assigned territory and actual control.
The final verse explicitly says Jebusites remain in Jerusalem, contradicting simplistic claims of complete dispossession and preserving a mixed-city reality.
safety_note: Do not use Joshua 15 as a modern border charter, to justify conquest, to treat women as rewards, or to erase Jebusite and Philistine inhabitants. Center Achsah's direct demand for life-giving water, the difference between allocation and possession, and the text's admission that Jerusalem remained shared.
